steps.title: Read the two data points from the table
steps.content: The table gives $$h(4) = 17$$ and $$h(6) = 23$$. These are the two points $$(4, 17)$$ and $$(6, 23)$$ on the line.

steps.title: Solve for the y-intercept
steps.content:
Using $$h(x) = mx + b$$ with $$m = 3$$ and the point $$(4, 17)$$:
$$17 = 3(4) + b \implies 17 = 12 + b \implies b = 5$$
So $$h(x) = 3x + 5$$. Check with the second point: $$h(6) = 3(6) + 5 = 23$$ ✓

steps.title: Eliminate wrong choices
steps.content:
• Choice A: $$h(x) = \frac{1}{3}x - \frac{5}{3}$$ — at $$x = 4$$, gives $$\frac{4}{3} - \frac{5}{3} = -\frac{1}{3} \ne 17$$. Eliminated.
  • Choice B: $$h(x) = 3x + 5$$ — gives $$h(4) = 17$$ and $$h(6) = 23$$. Correct.
  • Choice C: $$h(x) = 4x + 17$$ — slope is 4, but we found slope = 3. Eliminated.
  • Choice D: $$h(x) = 6x + 23$$ — slope is 6, not 3. Eliminated.
That matches choice B — B is correct.
